taxonomy: Metagonia ovata is classified within the family Pholcidae, which is a family of spiders commonly known as cellar spiders or daddy long legs. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. The family Pholcidae is further classified within the order Araneomorphae, which includes most modern spiders. Araneomorphae is a suborder of spiders within the class Arachnida, which also includes other arachnids like ticks and scorpions. Metagonia ovata is a species within the genus Metagonia, which is a genus of spiders found in the Americas.
